questions.question: What is the main difference between Propositional Logic and First Order Logic? (2-3 marks)
expectedPoints: First Order Logic is more expressive than Propositional Logic
First Order Logic allows us to represent knowledge about objects and their relationships

questions.question: Explain the syntax and semantics of the First Order Logic expression ∀x (Human(x) → Mortal(x)). (5-10 marks)
expectedPoints: The syntax of the expression consists of a universal quantifier, a variable, a predicate, and a logical operator
The semantics of the expression define the meaning of the expression in terms of a model, which is a set of objects and their relationships
The expression states that for all objects x, if x is human, then x is mortal
